description: Setup wizard prompts for Linear API key and appends api.linear.app to PRAXIS_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
steps: In praxis/setup_wizard.py, insert a new step after the GitHub step: ask 'Enable Linear integration? [y/N]'
If y: print instructions, use _getpass() for PRAXIS_LINEAR_API_KEY, write to .env, append api.linear.app to PRAXIS_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
Verify: test_linear_step_yes_writes_key_and_domain and test_linear_step_no_writes_nothing pass

description: Setup wizard prompts for Notion token and appends api.notion.com to PRAXIS_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
steps: In praxis/setup_wizard.py, insert step after wizard-linear-step: ask 'Enable Notion integration? [y/N]'
If y: print instructions for notion.so/my-integrations, use _getpass() for PRAXIS_NOTION_TOKEN, append api.notion.com
Verify: test_notion_step_yes_writes_token_and_domain passes

description: Setup wizard prompts for Calendar iCal URL and appends calendar.google.com to PRAXIS_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
steps: In praxis/setup_wizard.py, insert step after wizard-notion-step: ask 'Enable Calendar integration? [y/N]'
If y: print iCal URL instructions, use _input() (not _getpass) for PRAXIS_CALENDAR_URL, add to _redact_secrets(), append calendar.google.com
Verify: test_calendar_step_yes_writes_url and test_calendar_url_uses_input_not_getpass pass

description: PRAXIS_ALLOWED_DOMAINS appending is additive across all wizard steps — preserves existing, no duplicates
steps: Extract _append_allowed_domain(env_file, domain) helper: read current value, split by comma, add only if not present, write back
Use this helper in wizard-linear-step, wizard-notion-step, wizard-calendar-step
Verify: test_allowed_domains_additive_preserves_existing and test_allowed_domains_no_duplicate_on_rerun pass

description: Create praxis/event_bus.py with EventBus class, singleton, and event name constants
steps: Create praxis/event_bus.py: EventBus class with subscribe(), unsubscribe(), async publish(), publish_sync(); thread-safe with asyncio.Lock; queue maxsize=100; drop-on-full with stderr warning
Module-level constants: TASK_QUEUED, TASK_STARTED, TASK_TOKEN, TASK_COMPLETED, TASK_FAILED, APPROVAL_ADDED, SCHEDULE_FIRED, AMBIENT_TRIGGERED, COMPACTION_FIRED, HEARTBEAT
get_event_bus() returns process-level singleton; publish_sync handles both running-loop and no-loop cases
Verify: python -m pytest tests/test_event_bus.py -v — all 25+ tests pass

description: Wire TASK_STARTED/COMPLETED/FAILED events into queue_runner.py transition points
steps: In praxis/queue_runner.py _run_atomic_task(): emit TASK_STARTED after update_status('running'), TASK_COMPLETED after update_status('done'), TASK_FAILED after move_to_dead_letter()
In _run_staged_task(): emit TASK_STARTED after update_status('running'), TASK_COMPLETED after update_status('done'), TASK_FAILED after update_status('failed')
Add TODO comment above run_queue_loop(): 'TASK_QUEUED emitted from api.py POST /api/queue'
Verify: test_queue_runner_emits_started, test_queue_runner_emits_completed, test_queue_runner_emits_failed pass

description: Wire SCHEDULE_FIRED event into scheduler.py tick() after dispatching a task
steps: Read praxis/scheduler.py fully. In CronScheduler.tick(), after the TaskQueue.add() call, emit get_event_bus().publish_sync(SCHEDULE_FIRED, {schedule_id, name, prompt[:100]})
Verify: test_scheduler_emits_schedule_fired passes; existing scheduler tests still pass

description: Wire AMBIENT_TRIGGERED event into ambient.py source detection callbacks
steps: Read praxis/ambient.py fully. In each source callback (email, calendar, linear, github), after enqueuing a task, emit get_event_bus().publish_sync(AMBIENT_TRIGGERED, {source, summary[:200]})
Verify: existing ambient tests still pass; new test_ambient_emits_triggered passes

description: Create praxis/api.py with token auth helper; PRAXIS_UI_TOKEN env var for bearer auth
steps: Create praxis/api.py with _check_token(request) -> Response|None: if PRAXIS_UI_TOKEN not set return None; if Authorization != 'Bearer {token}' return 401 JSON
_check_token_ws(websocket) -> bool: same check for WebSocket handshake, uses query param ?token= or header
Add PRAXIS_UI_TOKEN and PRAXIS_MCP_BIND to .env.example under '# Web UI + API' section
Verify: test_api_no_auth_when_token_not_set and test_api_401_when_token_wrong pass

description: GET /api/status and GET /api/queue endpoints returning system info and paginated task list
steps: GET /api/status: return {version, queue_stats (counts by status from tasks.jsonl), daemon_running (praxis.pid exists and process alive)}
GET /api/queue: accept ?status=, ?limit=50, ?offset=0; read tasks.jsonl; return {tasks: [...], total: int}; each task has id, prompt_preview[:100], status, priority, queued_at
Verify: test_status_returns_json, test_queue_list_empty, test_queue_list_with_tasks pass

description: POST /api/queue (add task), GET /api/queue/{id} (detail), DELETE /api/queue/{id} (cancel)
steps: POST /api/queue: body {prompt, priority=3, mode='build'}; use TaskQueue.add(); emit TASK_QUEUED event; return {task_id}
GET /api/queue/{task_id}: return full task dict + result from results/{id}.txt if exists
DELETE /api/queue/{task_id}: cancel pending only (update_status 'failed', error='cancelled'); 404 if not found; 409 if not pending
Verify: test_queue_add_creates_task, test_queue_add_emits_event, test_queue_cancel_pending, test_queue_cancel_running_409 pass

description: GET /api/approvals list, POST approve/reject/bulk — scanning all staging files
steps: GET /api/approvals: scan .praxis/staging/external_actions.jsonl, slack/messages/*.json, events/*.ics, drafts/*.eml, wiki_updates.jsonl; return pending items as list
POST /api/approvals/{id}/approve and /reject: find item by id field across staging files; update status; reuse --approve code path from __main__.py
POST /api/approvals/bulk: {ids, action} — call approve/reject per id; return {results: [{id, status}]}
Verify: test_approvals_list_empty, test_approvals_list_with_external_actions, test_approval_approve_sets_status, test_approvals_bulk_approve_all pass

description: Full CRUD for /api/schedule: list, add, update, delete, enable, disable, run-now
steps: GET /api/schedule: read .praxis/schedule/tasks.json; return list of ScheduledTask dicts
POST /api/schedule: add via CronScheduler.add_task(); PUT /{id}: update fields; DELETE /{id}: remove
POST /{id}/enable, /{id}/disable: toggle enabled; POST /{id}/run-now: enqueue task immediately
Verify: test_schedule_list, test_schedule_add, test_schedule_enable_disable, test_schedule_run_now pass

description: Wiki search/list/detail, memory list, integration validate, soul/heartbeat GET/PUT
steps: GET /api/wiki/search?q=: keyword search in wiki/pages/*.md filenames and content; return [{slug, score, content_preview}]
GET /api/wiki/pages and /api/wiki/pages/{slug}: list with frontmatter, detail with full content
GET /api/memory: last 10 entries from .praxis/memory/conversations/*.jsonl
GET /api/integrations (cached, TTL=300s) and POST /api/integrations/validate (force fresh): call validate logic, return [{name, status, message}]
GET/PUT /api/soul and /api/heartbeat: read/write .praxis/SOUL.md and .praxis/HEARTBEAT.md
Verify: test_wiki_search_matches_slug, test_wiki_page_detail_404, test_integrations_uses_cache pass

description: WS /ws WebSocket endpoint subscribing to EventBus and streaming events to connected clients
steps: In praxis/mcp_server.py start(): add ws_endpoint(websocket) async function that accepts WebSocket, subscribes to EventBus '*', loops await queue.get() with 25s timeout (send ping on timeout), sends events as JSON, unsubscribes on disconnect
Add 'from starlette.routing import WebSocketRoute' and 'from starlette.websockets import WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect' to imports inside try block
Add WebSocketRoute('/ws', endpoint=ws_endpoint) to the Starlette routes list
Verify: test_ws_auth_rejected_without_token, test_ws_receives_events pass

description: Register all api.py routes in mcp_server.py, parameterize host binding, serve UI static files
steps: Import all handler functions from praxis.api and add their Routes to the Starlette routes list in start()
Change uvicorn.run host='127.0.0.1' to host=os.environ.get('PRAXIS_MCP_BIND','127.0.0.1')
After routes list, conditionally Mount('/ui', StaticFiles(directory=str(ui_dist), html=True)) if praxis/ui/dist/ exists
Update startup stderr log to print API, WebSocket, and UI URLs
Verify: python -m praxis --mcp starts; curl http://localhost:8765/api/status returns JSON

description: Create praxis/ui/ Vite+React project scaffold with package.json, vite.config.ts, tailwind, index.html
steps: Create praxis/ui/package.json with react 18, react-dom, react-router-dom, @tanstack/react-query, zustand, date-fns, react-markdown, remark-gfm, lucide-react; devDeps: vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, tailwindcss, autoprefixer, postcss
Create vite.config.ts: base='/ui/', outDir='dist', proxy /api/* and /ws to localhost:8765
Create tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.ts, index.html mounting #root
Add praxis/ui/node_modules/ and praxis/ui/dist/ to .gitignore
Verify: cd praxis/ui && npm install && npm run build completes without errors

description: Global CSS design tokens: dark theme, color palette, typography, layout primitives
steps: Create src/styles/globals.css: --bg:#0a0c10, --surface:#13161f, --surface2:#1c2030, --border:rgba(255,255,255,0.08), --text1:#e8ecf4, --text2:#8892a4, --cyan:#22d3ee, --green:#4ade80, --orange:#fb923c, --red:#f87171
CSS classes: .chip (status pill), .status-dot, @keyframes pulse, @keyframes blink
Verify: npm run build succeeds; design tokens appear in compiled output

description: API client fetch wrapper and Zustand WebSocket store connecting to /ws
steps: Create src/api/client.ts: get(path), post(path, body), del(path); adds Authorization: Bearer header if VITE_PRAXIS_TOKEN env set; throws ApiError with status on non-2xx

Create src/store/ws.ts: Zustand store with {connected, pendingApprovals, runningTasks, connect(url), disconnect()}; on 'approval.added' event increment pendingApprovals; on 'task.started' increment runningTasks; on 'task.completed'/'task.failed' decrement
Verify: TypeScript compiles; npm run build succeeds

description: App router with React Router and sidebar Layout with nav badges
steps: Create src/App.tsx: BrowserRouter with routes / → Chat, /approvals → Approvals, /tasks → Tasks, /wiki → Wiki, /wiki/:slug → WikiPage, /schedule → Schedule, /settings → Settings; initialize WebSocket on mount

Create src/components/Layout.tsx: fixed-width sidebar (52px) with icon nav buttons using lucide-react; orange badge on Approvals showing pendingApprovals from ws store; cyan badge on Tasks showing runningTasks
Verify: npm run build succeeds; http://localhost:5173/ui/ loads in dev mode

description: Chat screen: task input, mode toggle, streaming output panel, task history sidebar
steps: Create src/screens/Chat.tsx: left sidebar (200px) with recent tasks from GET /api/queue?limit=20; each item shows prompt_preview, status chip, time ago
Main panel: output area (flex-1, monospace, scrollable) with prompt line in cyan and output text; animated blinking cursor while running; renders markdown on completion via react-markdown
Input row: multiline textarea (Ctrl+Enter submits), mode pill toggle Build/Plan, send button; POST /api/queue on submit; quick-action pills: 'Morning briefing', 'Lint wiki', 'Queue status'
WebSocket: subscribe to task.token events for active task_id, append to output; on task.completed stop cursor and render markdown
Verify: submit a task, see streaming output, task appears in history sidebar

description: Approvals screen: card list with approve/reject, bulk actions, browser notifications
steps: Create src/screens/Approvals.tsx: fetch GET /api/approvals; refetch on approval.added WebSocket event

Per-card: provider icon (N for Notion, colored dot for Linear, mail for email), action name, time ago, checkbox; params in monospace dark block; Approve (green) / Reject (red) buttons; on action: fade card, decrement badge
Bulk bar (shown when items selected): 'N items selected' + 'Approve selected' + 'Reject selected'; calls POST /api/approvals/bulk
Empty state: checkmark icon + 'All clear — nothing needs your approval'
Browser notifications: request permission on mount; send Notification on approval.added when page not focused
Verify: pending approvals appear; approve/reject updates state; badge decrements

description: Task Board: 4-column kanban (Pending/Running/Done/Failed), task detail drawer
steps: Create src/screens/TaskBoard.tsx: 4-column CSS grid; fetch GET /api/queue on load; refetch on task.* WebSocket events
Pending column: cards with prompt_preview, priority badge, 'queued N min ago'; Cancel button (×) → DELETE /api/queue/{id}
Running column: pulsing cyan dot in header; cards with elapsed timer (update every 5s); animated left border
Done column: green header badge; Failed column: red header badge with error_preview; Retry button → POST /api/queue re-submit same prompt
Task detail drawer: slides in from right on card click; full prompt, full result (markdown), error, retry history; close button
Verify: tasks appear in correct columns; running tasks show elapsed time; cancel removes card

description: Wiki Browser: entity list with search, markdown page viewer with frontmatter, ingest button
steps: Create src/screens/Wiki.tsx: search bar → GET /api/wiki/search?q=; left panel entity list from GET /api/wiki/pages grouped by type (People/Projects/Other); click → navigate to /wiki/:slug

Create src/screens/WikiPage.tsx: GET /api/wiki/pages/:slug; render with react-markdown + remark-gfm; frontmatter badges (valid_from, entity_type); yellow superseded banner if superseded_on set; typed links as clickable pills
Ingest button: file picker → POST /api/wiki/ingest (if endpoint exists, else show TODO toast)
Verify: navigate to Wiki, search for a page slug, see rendered markdown

description: Schedule Manager: table with enable/disable toggles, add form with cron preview
steps: Create src/screens/Schedule.tsx: fetch GET /api/schedule; table with Name, Cron, Next run, Last run, Enabled toggle, Actions
Toggle: POST /api/schedule/{id}/enable or /disable; Run now: POST /api/schedule/{id}/run-now with brief 'queued' toast; Delete: with confirmation popover
Add form (shown on '+ Add'): name input, cron input, prompt textarea; human-readable cron preview from simple lookup table (e.g. '0 7 * * *' → 'Every day at 7am'); Submit → POST /api/schedule
Verify: schedules appear; toggle updates enabled state; add form creates schedule

description: Settings: integration health panel, runtime info, SOUL.md and HEARTBEAT.md editors
steps: Create src/screens/Settings.tsx: 2-column grid of cards
Card 1 — Integration Health: GET /api/integrations; per-row: name, status icon (green check/red X/gray minus), message; Refresh button → POST /api/integrations/validate
Card 2 — Runtime Info: GET /api/status; show version, runtime, model, uptime, queue depth in monospace
Card 3 (full width) — SOUL.md: GET /api/soul; textarea; Save → PUT /api/soul
Card 4 (full width) — HEARTBEAT.md: same for /api/heartbeat
Verify: integrations show current status; SOUL.md content loads and saves

description: Wire npm run build into install.sh; add UI to .gitignore; update startup log
steps: Add to install.sh: if node available, cd praxis/ui && npm install && npm run build; else print Node.js not found message
Verify .gitignore has praxis/ui/node_modules/ and praxis/ui/dist/
Verify: bash install.sh from fresh checkout builds the UI; python -m praxis --mcp shows 'UI: http://127.0.0.1:8765/ui/' in startup log

description: Create praxis/trust.py with TrustStore and TrustLevel enum
steps: Create praxis/trust.py: TrustLevel(IntEnum) with OWNER=0, ADMIN=1, USER=2, BLOCKED=3

TrustStore.__init__(config: dict): reads PRAXIS_OWNER_IDS and PRAXIS_ADMIN_IDS (format 'channel:id,channel:id') from env; reads convergence.yaml channels.<ch>.trust_ids.owner/admin lists; PRAXIS_DEFAULT_TRUST default='user'
resolve(channel, sender_id) -> TrustLevel: lookup order: env vars → yaml → default
is_read_only_prompt(prompt) -> bool: any keyword in {'query','status','validate','what','show','list','search','tell me','how many','when','who','where','check'} in prompt.lower()
get_trust_store(config) singleton; add PRAXIS_OWNER_IDS/PRAXIS_ADMIN_IDS/PRAXIS_DEFAULT_TRUST to .env.example
Verify: python -m pytest tests/test_trust.py -v — 25+ tests pass

description: Wire TrustStore into Telegram, Slack, and WhatsApp message handlers
steps: In praxis/integrations/telegram.py message handler: resolve trust for update.effective_user.id; BLOCKED→return silently; USER+write-intent prompt→reply 'I can only answer read-only queries'; ADMIN/OWNER→proceed
In praxis/slack_listener.py: same pattern using event['user'] as sender_id
In praxis/integrations/whatsapp.py: add trust check on top of ALLOWED_NUMBERS; OWNER_IDS format 'whatsapp:number'
Add trust_ids section documentation comment to convergence.yaml
Verify: test_telegram_handler_blocks_message, test_telegram_handler_allows_owner pass; all existing channel tests pass

description: Add context compaction to ClaudeCodeRuntime: token tracking and _maybe_compact()
steps: Read praxis/runtime/claude_code.py fully. Add _MODEL_CONTEXT_LIMITS dict and _DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LIMIT=128_000
Add self._session_tokens=0 to __init__; after each API response add input+output tokens to _session_tokens

Add _maybe_compact(messages) -> list: if _session_tokens >= max_ctx * threshold, append compaction prompt, call LLM once, rebuild history (system + summary + last 8 pairs), reset tokens, emit COMPACTION_FIRED event; return new messages
Wire _maybe_compact() into run_loop() after token update; add PRAXIS_COMPACTION_THRESHOLD and PRAXIS_COMPACTION_RESERVE to .env.example
Verify: tests/test_compaction.py — triggered at threshold, not below, history rebuilt correctly

description: Same context compaction logic for OpenAIBaseRuntime
steps: Read praxis/runtime/openai_base.py fully. Apply same _maybe_compact() pattern as compaction-claude-runtime
For OpenAI models, use 128_000 as default context limit; read model name from self._model or equivalent
Verify: test_openai_runtime_compaction_triggered passes; all existing OpenAI runtime tests pass

description: Create praxis/memory_store.py with SemanticMemoryStore using ChromaDB and sentence-transformers
steps: Create praxis/memory_store.py: SemanticMemoryStore class with ChromaDB persistent client at .praxis/memory/chroma/; embedding model all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via sentence-transformers
embed_wiki_page(slug, content, metadata): upsert by slug; set metadata.active=False if superseded_on set
search(query, top_k=6, include_superseded=False) -> list[dict]: return [{slug, score, content_preview[:200], metadata}]; return [] silently if deps missing
delete_page(slug), rebuild_index(wiki_root) -> int
get_memory_store(workspace_root) -> SemanticMemoryStore|None: return None if PRAXIS_SEMANTIC_MEMORY != 'true' or deps missing
Add [semantic] dep group: chromadb>=0.4, sentence-transformers>=2.2; add .praxis/memory/chroma/ to .gitignore
Verify: tests/test_memory_store.py with mocked chromadb — all 20+ tests pass

description: Wire semantic memory into wiki.py ingest() and orchestrator context assembly; add CLI flag
steps: In praxis/wiki.py ingest(): after writing page to disk, call get_memory_store(wiki_root).embed_wiki_page() if store exists
In praxis/orchestrator.py context assembly (before LLM call): call get_memory_store().search(prompt, top_k) and prepend as '## Relevant memory\n' block AFTER §5 governance block
In praxis/__main__.py: add --rebuild-memory-index flag that calls store.rebuild_index(wiki_root) and prints count
Add PRAXIS_SEMANTIC_MEMORY=true, PRAXIS_SEMANTIC_TOP_K=6 to .env.example
Verify: test_wiki_ingest_calls_embed, test_orchestrator_prepends_semantic_context pass

description: Create praxis/hooks_engine.py with FileWatcher using watchdog library
steps: Create praxis/hooks_engine.py: FileWatcher class reading PRAXIS_WATCH_PATHS, PRAXIS_WATCH_EXTENSIONS, PRAXIS_WATCH_DEBOUNCE (default 5s), PRAXIS_WATCH_PROMPT_TEMPLATE
_PraxisEventHandler(FileSystemEventHandler): on CREATE events matching extension: debounce by path, enqueue Task with expanded template prompt, priority=5
FileWatcher.start() → background daemon thread with watchdog Observer; stop() → graceful stop
IMPORTANT: watcher never reads file content — only generates a prompt asking orchestrator to use Read tool
Add [hooks] dep group: watchdog>=3.0; add PRAXIS_WATCH_PATHS section to .env.example
Verify: test_file_watcher_enqueues_on_create, test_file_watcher_ignores_non_matching_extension pass

description: Add webhook receiver route to hooks_engine.py and wire both into daemon and mcp_server
steps: In praxis/hooks_engine.py: add make_webhook_routes() returning list of Starlette Routes for POST /webhooks/{source}; handler validates HMAC-SHA256 if PRAXIS_WEBHOOK_SECRET_{SOURCE} set; reads JSON body (max 64KB); enqueues Task with prompt 'Webhook from {source}: {json[:500]}'
In praxis/daemon.py or queue_runner.py after _start_ambient_monitor: if PRAXIS_WATCH_PATHS set, start FileWatcher
In praxis/mcp_server.py start(): call make_webhook_routes() and extend routes list
Verify: test_webhook_valid_json_enqueues_task, test_webhook_invalid_hmac_returns_401 pass

description: Create praxis/voice.py with VoiceTranscriber supporting API (Whisper) and local backends
steps: Create praxis/voice.py: VoiceTranscriptionError(Exception); VoiceTranscriber(backend='api'): API backend uses openai.Audio.transcriptions.create(model='whisper-1'); local backend uses faster-whisper (optional)
transcribe(audio_path, language='en') -> str: raises VoiceTranscriptionError on failure; supports .ogg,.mp3,.m4a,.wav,.webm
get_transcriber() -> VoiceTranscriber|None: singleton if PRAXIS_VOICE_ENABLED=true; None otherwise
Add [voice] dep group: openai>=1.0; add PRAXIS_VOICE_ENABLED, PRAXIS_WHISPER_BACKEND, PRAXIS_WHISPER_LANGUAGE to .env.example
Verify: tests/test_voice.py with mocked openai — test_transcriber_returns_none_when_disabled, test_transcribe_calls_whisper_api pass

description: Wire voice transcription into Telegram adapter for voice message handling
steps: Read praxis/integrations/telegram.py fully. In message handler, check if update.message.voice or .audio is set before processing text

If voice: get_transcriber(); if None reply 'Voice input not enabled. Set PRAXIS_VOICE_ENABLED=true.'; else download file to tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.ogg'), transcribe, unlink, set message_text='[Voice] {transcribed}'
On VoiceTranscriptionError: reply 'Voice transcription failed: {e}' and return
Verify: test_telegram_handler_routes_voice_to_transcriber, test_telegram_handler_prepends_voice_tag pass
